actor.name: Patrick Henry Gallen
actor.description: Senior Advisor and Futurist at Copenhagen Institute for Futures Studies

data.commentary:
Efter över ett års kamp – först som individ och sedan som talesperson för Fair Transition – går Tidöregeringens proposition om skärpta krav för svenskt medborgarskap till omröstning i riksdagen på onsdag den 29 april. Att lagen kommer att röstas igenom är i princip helt säkert. Hur den kommer att se ut är det inte.

Tack vare tusentals engagerade och hårt arbetande människor runt om i landet finns fortfarande en möjlighet att påverka utfallet. Om bara ett fåtal ledamöter från Liberalerna väljer att stå upp för den svenska rättsstaten och en rättvis migrationspolitik tillsammans med oppositionen, kan övergångsbestämmelser säkras. Då skulle de nya reglerna endast gälla ansökningar som lämnas in efter den 6 juni.

Utan sådana övergångsregler – som både regeringens egna utredare och Lagrådet har rekommenderat – riskerar 100 000 människor att få avslag på deras ansökningar. Människor som i många fall har väntat i över fyra år på besked från Migrationsverket (Swedish Migration Agency).

Sverige riskerar dessutom att bli det ENDA landet i Europa som inför skärpta medborgarskapskrav utan övergångsbestämmelser. Det underminerar rättsstaten, urholkar tilliten till våra institutioner och skadar vår förmåga att attrahera och behålla den internationella kompetens som vår ekonomi är beroende av.

Jag påverkas inte själv av de nya reglerna. Jag hade turen att bli svensk medborgare i oktober – den viktigaste händelsen i mitt liv. Men jag har inte glömt den sårbarhet, den förlamande ångest och den totala maktlöshet jag kände som icke-EU-medborgare varje dag innan dess. Och jag kommer aldrig att glömma den livsavgörande lättnad och trygghet som medborgarskapet innebar.

När jag fick mitt medborgarskap lovade jag mig själv att aldrig glömma – och att aldrig sluta kämpa för dem som är mest politiskt och juridiskt utsatta. Jag kommer alltid att använda de rättigheter och privilegier som det lilla röda passet ger för att verka för ett mer rättvist samhälle.

Om man blir svensk medborgare innan den 6 juni ska inte vara en fråga om tur - och därför måste den nya lagen införas med rimliga övergångsbestämmelser.

Nu har våra politiker i riksdagen 48 timmar kvar att avgöra om de står upp för rättsstaten – och för ett Sverige byggt på tillit och öppenhet, snarare än rädsla, kortsiktighet och symbolpolitik.

Jag hoppas att de fattar rätt beslut.
The Local Europe Becky Waterton Emma Löfgren Cecilia Rönn Malin Danielsson Niels Paarup-Petersen

data.commentary:
Never waste a good excuse to play with new tech.
This weekend’s “productivity tool” (that absolutely does not improve my productivity): the Starlink Mini.

My quick at‑home measurements:

• Link speed: 242 MB/s
• Download: 105 MB/s
• Upload: 23.4MB/s


While these speeds won’t replace traditional Wi‑Fi or 5G, the real value lies elsewhere: reliable internet access in locations with zero cellular coverage. For anyone working in remote environments—field research, outdoor production, emergency response, or simply “work from anywhere”—this is a meaningful shift.

I’m planning to test it in more challenging environments soon.
If someone has an RV（Husbil）to lend, I’m more than happy to take this experiment on the road.

If you’re curious about Starlink, use this link to start your journey and we could all get one month free of using
👉 https://lnkd.in/dPtGuBpT

And if you’re in Gothenburg, you’re welcome to stop by and see it in action.

#Starlink #Connectivity #RemoteWork #TechLife #SatelliteInternet #WorkFromAnywhere #Gothenburg

data.commentary:
The comparison process promises to be fascinating! While unstructured data is being "organized" by LLMs, do they actually perform better with faster response times in RAG scenarios?  

We’re taking inspiration from Andrej Karpathy and putting it to the test—let’s see if the way we construct our RAG systems really makes a difference and sense.

The performance is not just about speed. What if we could “enrich” the knowledge base itself by having LLMs generate novel, context-aware answers?  

Imagine this: instead of just retrieving facts, we’re “creating”insights—like smashing together breads, noodles, and pastas into a creative new dish, tailored exactly to the question at hand. That’s the future of RAG: not just retrieval, but intelligent synthesis.

Resource：https://lnkd.in/eSzCYjZ2

#RAG #LLM #LargeLanguageModels #AIResearch #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #DataScience #Innovation

actor.name: Harvad Li
actor.description: Agentic AI Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
From the dust on the laptops, you can tell which one gets the most love.

The first one? My #PinebookPro. Last opened nearly half a year ago. The angry emojis? That’s from when I helped my daughter review math exam questions — let’s just say she wasn’t impressed. 🙃  
Truth is, without solid app support and ecosystem maturity, it’s been gathering dust more than doing real work. Now I've installed Kali on it — it's almost the best Linux OS for this device.

The second: a #LenovoThinkpadE15 Gen 3 running Windows 11. I open it maybe once a month — mostly to test a project on another platform or upload something to Google Drive. It does its job, but it’s not my go-to. 

And then there’s the third: my #MacminiM4Pro — 64GB RAM, 2TB SSD. Running 24/7 as my #homelab. No dust. No breaks. This is the one I actually live on.

So — what about you?  
Which machine at home do you actually *use most*, and why?  
Let’s swap setups and stories.

Happy weekend, everyone!

#MacMini #M4Pro #PinebookPro #HomeLab #AppleSilicon #LinuxEcosystem #DevSetup #LenovoE15 #ARMComputing #HomeOffice

data.commentary:
It was minus 12°C this morning. After dropping off my kid, I headed straight to Jula for #birdfood — only to find it sold out. Same at Rusta and Bauhaus in Sisjön.  

Finally picked some up at Biltema. But what stayed with me wasn’t the cold — it was seeing a man walking out with both arms full of seed bags, like he wasn’t just feeding his own birds, but half the forest.  

In weather like this, it means something to know so many people are quietly doing their part. No fanfare, no posts — just care, one handful at a time. Every little bit helps them survive the freeze. 🐦❄️  

#SilentLove #WarmsWinter #BirdLovers #Birdfood #CommunityLove #NatureInWinter

data.commentary:
Now hosting the invoice printing system, New Year Project No. 3, on my VPS, running as a live solution. The next step is integrating with the SEB API to automatically fetch and print monthly transaction histories, including #bankgiro transactions, multiple times a day. This setup solves a real pain point—efficient, low-cost automation—with almost zero overhead compared to expensive market alternatives.

The frontend is hosted on Cloudflare Pages, and the backend runs on my VPS, making it a fast, reliable, and completely free stack for me. It’s already saving me over 30 minutes each month on manual tasks.
I’m considering releasing this tool to the market at a fair price. If you're running a small or medium-sized business, how much would you be willing to pay for a tool that saves you 30–40 minutes every month on invoice sorting and printing then?

#CloudFlarePages #VPS #OCR #InvoiceSys #TimeSaving #LLM

article.title: New Year, New Project No. 6: SSH From Anywhere — With Zero Exposed Ports
article.subtitle: Harvad Li

actor.name: Harvad Li
actor.description: Agentic AI Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
New Year New Project: No.6 — SSH From Anywhere ��� With Zero Exposed Ports

This initiative focuses on enhancing security through a zero-trust approach, utilizing tools like Cloudflare Zero Trust and Tailscale. It aims to improve remote access capabilities while ensuring robust cybersecurity measures are in place.

Key components of the project include:
- Leveraging SSH for secure connections
- Ensuring no ports are exposed to the internet
- Implementing mobile-first solutions for access on devices like iPhone
- Utilizing tmux for efficient session management
- Configuring a Mac Mini as a remote access server

Looking forward to sharing insights and progress on this journey! 

#NewYearNewProject #ZeroTrust #CloudflareZeroTrust #SSH #Tailscale #DevOps #HomeLab #CyberSecurity #tmux #RemoteAccess #1.1.1.1 #iPhone #Termius #MacMini #SysAdmin #SRE #CloudComputing

data.commentary:
Testing isn't boring when you're ensuring quality for every user.

Even for personal projects, user experience matters. So I did what needed to be done: tested all 182 models on NVIDIA's NIM platform.

The results? ✅ 113 out of 182 models (62%) work perfectly with chat and conversation ❌ 69 require paid tier or special access

This means users of my project Nividia-nim-swift can access the demo: models.bluehawana.com confidently choose from 113 verified working models - no more guessing which ones will fail.

Why this matters:

Transparency builds trust
Users save time (no trial and error)
Clear documentation = better UX
Even side projects deserve quality assurance
The full compatibility report is now in the GitHub repo, so anyone forking or starring the project knows exactly what works.

Because good testing isn't about perfection - it's about knowing your product inside out and being honest about it.


#AI #OpenSource #QualityAssurance #NVIDIA #DeveloperTools #BuildInPublic

article.title: Beyond Copilot: Free Access to 182 Cutting-Edge AI Models on NVIDIA—My New Year Project No.5
article.subtitle: Harvad Li

actor.name: Harvad Li
actor.description: Agentic AI Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
New Year, New Project: No.5 — Claude Code Proxy with NVIDIA Models

What if cost wasn't the barrier to exploring the world's most advanced AI models?
That's what inspired me to launch Project No.5. After completing projects 1-4, I wanted to take on a bigger challenge: making high-end #AI accessible to anyone curious enough to build.
Most developers never get to test top-tier models because they're too "expensive". Comparing them? Even harder. So people stick with familiar tools like #Copilot, missing out on models that could be faster, sharper, or better suited for specific tasks.
The game needs to be changed now.
Introducing the #NVIDIANIMProxySwitch —a tool that lets you switch between 182 NVIDIA NIM models in under a second. No restarts, no config edits, no hassle.
Here's what it offers:
- Instant model switching (under 1 second)
- 182 models ready to explore
- Web UI and REST API
- Zero downtime
- Seamless integration with Claude Code
- Fully open source
Built entirely with Qwen Coder via this project and Kiro, this project is AI helping build AI infrastructure.
And it’s completely free to use with NVIDIA’s NIM API, so you never have to choose between your budget and your curiosity.
Want to give it a try? Here is the demo for 7 days : models.bluehawana.com
Would like to have a deep understanding, you can find the repo in the article below. 
Feel free to star it, save it, or open a PR if you’d like to contribute.
I'm always excited to see what people create when limits disappear.
Full write-up below: applying API, architecture, testing, setup, and how to get started.
Project No.5 is just the beginning. It's still early in the year—plenty more to build.

#AI #MachineLearning #NVIDIA #OpenSource #Python #DeveloperTools #NewYearNewProject

article.title: "Can Not Live Without AI?" ---- Reflections on AI Coding Tools Adoption in Corporation Level
article.subtitle: Harvad Li

actor.name: Harvad Li
actor.description: Agentic AI Developer @ Volvo Group | Cloud-Native (Kubernetes, Microservices) | Cloud Architect | DevOps | FinOps

data.commentary:
Yesterday I went to a Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) meetup about AI in development at Spotify. Great event, lots of energy. But afterwards, I noticed something that's been bothering me.
Almost every Swedish company I talk to: Windows Laptop, Microsoft stack, VS Code, GitHub Copilot. That's it.
When I ask "Why not try Claude, Gemini or others on Mac?", the answer is always: "data security concerns."
But here's the thing - most companies already pay for Microsoft and Azure, so they add Copilot for $20/month and feel safe because "Microsoft will protect our data." Is that really security thinking? Or just going with what feels familiar?
Here's What Nobody's Talking About :
Some of the best coding models right now are Chinese: #Qwen-3.5-coder (Alibaba), #GLM-4.7 (Zhipu), #MiniMax 2.1 (Xiyu), #Kimi 2.5 (Moonshot). They're top-ranked on Hugging Face and NVIDIA platforms. In many coding tasks, they're genuinely better than Copilot. Significantly better.
And it's not just Chinese models - Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini - nobody's even trying them.
From my experience testing these tools extensively? #Copilot wouldn't even make my top 10. I'm not exaggerating.
My Suggestion :
Look, if you're scared that Chinese AI models will send your data back to China(Maybe not) - fine, I understand that concern. But at least try other American AI giants like #Anthropic's #ClaudeCode, #GoogleGemini & #Antigravity, #AWS #Kiro, or even #OpenAI's #Codex directly instead of staying trapped within Copilot.
Unless you have factory software locked into Microsoft, why not explore what's actually best for your team?
Most of these models are easy to access: Ollama (local) or on cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs, NVIDIA catalog, many with free tiers. Take your actual code, run tests, compare. Then decide based on performance, not on "this is what other Swedish companies use."
A Personal Note
I'm from China, working in tech here in Sweden. I love it here. But sometimes there's this automatic assumption: Chinese technology = risky, American technology = safe. That's not how technology works.
Funny timing though - with all the #Greenland drama lately, suddenly Chinese companies seem a bit more "acceptable" in Europe :) 
Maybe it takes geopolitical chaos to remind us that technological diversity isn't just nice to have - it's strategic.
What worries me: what if the US restricts AI services due to geopolitics one day? Shouldn't we at least know what alternatives exist?
The "Can't Live Without AI" theme from yesterday's meetup is true. AI is part of our work now.
But let's make sure we're choosing the right tools, not just the comfortable ones.
Maybe I'm overthinking this. Maybe I'm too sensitive about the China thing. But I genuinely think we're missing out on better tools because of assumptions we haven't questioned. What do you think? Read the article for more details.

#Antigravity #Kiro #ClaudeCode #DevTools #CNCF #Sweden #CodingIDE #AIModels #Huggingface #Nvidia

data.commentary:
I bet Pencil by TomKrcha will change how you think about #UXUI design. It’s a fresh, interactive tool that lets you design and edit websites, dashboards, and interfaces in real time — either by typing prompts or by manually tweaking elements. Think of it as a live, hands‑on rethink of the design workflow: build something with a prompt, then refine it instantly with direct manipulation.
Why it feels different
Real‑time iteration — no long handoffs between design and prototyping; you see changes as you make them.
Prompt + polish — start with natural language, then fine‑tune visually; great for rapid exploration.
Not a Figma clone — it’s an alternative mindset: more interactive, more experimental, more immediate.
Short learning curve, big payoff — it takes a minute to get used to, but once you do it speeds up ideation and reduces friction.
Try it
Give www.pencil.dev a spin — play with a prompt, tweak the UI, and see how fast you can go from idea to a working interface. If you’re into fast iteration and new design vibes, this one’s worth your time. 

Check out Tom Krcha's post on X to learn more about Pencil and its potential to transform UX/UI design: 
Resource: https://lnkd.in/erc_A7XB

#DesignTools #UXUI #PencilDev
